request.question: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?

title: WrestleMania 29
Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song)
Luke Gallows
WWE Slam City
King of the Ring (2010)
Elimination Chamber (2013)
Royal Rumble (2013)
Judgment of Princeton
Cult of Personality (song)
WrestleMania XXX
sentences: WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.
"Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.
The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.
The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.
The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.
The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute
Andrew William Hankinson (born December 22, 1983) is an American professional wrestler and promoter, currently signed with WWE on the Raw brand under the ring name Luke Gallows.

During his first stint with the promotion, Hankinson also appeared briefly as an imposter of the wrestler Kane during 2006 and then returned as Festus, a seemingly mentally handicapped character who teamed with Jesse (Ray Gordy), but the team achieved little success.

After disappearing from television, Hankinson later became the enforcer and "disciple" of CM Punk, sporting a cleaner, militant look, with the Festus character's "real identity" revealed as Luke Gallows.
His first run in WWE ended in 2010.

He is perhaps best known for working for New Japan Pro Wrestling under the ring name Doc Gallows, and he also wrestled prominently under the TNA banner, where he was a member of the heel Aces & Eights stable under the ring name D.O.C. (Director of Chaos).

Along with Karl Anderson, Hankinson has won the WWE Raw Tag Team Championship once since returning to WWE, and he also held the IWGP Tag Team Championship three times with Anderson, making them one of only three tag teams (with The Steiner Brothers and The Dudley Boyz) to have held both titles between those promotions.
WWE Slam City was an animated show produced by WWE, which aired on the WWE Network and Wednesday evenings on Nicktoons.
WWE Slam City was based on the Mattel action figure series starring toyetic versions of the WWE roster circa 2013.

The series, filmed in the next generation of stop-motion animation, features a new WWE animated character The Finisher, who fires all of the WWE wrestlers and sends them to Slam City to find day jobs.
The wrestlers are plunged into new career challenges as they pack every street corner with work to do and scores to settle.

With John Cena as an auto mechanic, Rey Mysterio as a traffic guard, Alberto Del Rio as a coffee house barista, Mark Henry as a pizza shop mascot, CM Punk as an ice-cream man, Dolph Ziggler as an industry leader, Randy Orton as a zookeeper, Kane as a chef and Sheamus as a theater usher.
Daniel Bryan, The Miz, Big Show, Brock Lesnar, Santino Marella and Damien Sandow are still jobless but Santino and Sandow are the most desperate to find one.
WWE Legends Stone Cold and The Rock are also featured in Slam City.
The 'Stretch Move' feature of Slam City characters is highlighted in WWE Slam City.
The 2010 King of the Ring was a special edition of "Raw".
that aired on November 29, 2010, at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
The program featured the nineteenth King of the Ring tournament and first since 2008.
Sheamus defeated John Morrison in the finals of the tournament to become King of the Ring.

Unlike the previous tournament that featured wrestlers from the "Raw," "SmackDown" and "ECW" brands, the 2010 version of the tournament only featured wrestlers from "Raw" and "SmackDown" (following the closure of the ECW brand in February 2010).

The last King of the Ring tournament which only featured wrestlers from both "Raw" and "SmackDown" was held in 2002 (following the WWE Brand Extension the previous month, but before the ECW brand was introduced as a third brand in 2006).

Despite the event being mainly focused on the King of the Ring concept, the main event featured Jerry Lawler (who was celebrating his birthday that day) facing the new WWE Champion The Miz for the WWE Championship in a Tables, Ladders and Chairs match which marked Lawler's first WWE Championship match.
Lawler lost due to distractions from Alex Riley and Michael Cole.
CM Punk was joined in commentary on the show.
Elimination Chamber (2013) (also known as No Escape (2013) in Germany) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on February 17, 2013, at the New Orleans Arena in New Orleans, Louisiana.
The pay-per-view was the fourth annual Elimination Chamber event.
This year's event was headlined by The Rock defending the WWE Championship against CM Punk.
Royal Rumble (2013) was the twenty-sixth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on January 27, 2013 at the US Airways Center in Phoenix, Arizona.
It was the first WWE pay-per-view of 2013, and featured John Cena winning the traditional Royal Rumble match.
In the main event, The Rock defeated CM Punk for the WWE Championship, recapturing the title for the first time since 2002, and ending Punk's long reign at 434 days.

The Judgment of Princeton was a wine tasting (or blind tasting) event held on 8 June 2012 during a conference of the American Association of Wine Economists held at Princeton University in Princeton, New Jersey.

The purpose of this event was to compare, by a blind tasting, of several French wines against wines produced in New Jersey in order to gauge the quality and development of the New Jersey wine industry.
Because New Jersey's wine industry is relatively young and small, it has received little attention in the world wine market.

The state's wine production has experienced growth in recent years largely as a result of state legislators offering new opportunities for winery licensing and repealing Prohibition-era laws that have constrained the industry's development in past years.

This event was modeled after a 1976 blind tasting event dubbed the "Judgment of Paris" in which French wines were compared to several wines produced in California when that state's wine industry was similarly young and developing.
The New Jersey wine industry heralded the results and asserted that the rating of New Jersey wines by the blind tasting's judges was a victory for the state's wine industry.
"Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.
It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.
"Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.
It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.
Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.
The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".
The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.
It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".
The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".
In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".
The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".
It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".
The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.
The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".
WrestleMania XXX (also written as WrestleMania 30) was the thirtieth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on April 6, 2014, at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans, Louisiana.
It was the first WWE event simultaneously broadcast live on pay-per-view television and WWE's new streaming media service, the WWE Network.
Seven professional wrestling matches were scheduled on the event's card and one pre-show match was streamed on the WWE Network.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Disambiguation for short/ambiguous title tokens:
- If an anchor in summary_1 is a short or potentially ambiguous title token, do not automatically append an entertainment-domain keyword. Only append a plain entertainment disambiguator keyword (for example: film movie album song novel author director) when the original question itself contains explicit entertainment-role words (e.g., film, movie, album, song, directed, author, novella) or when the question explicitly lists multiple title-like tokens. In those cases, include the visible anchor(s) from summary_1 plus the appropriate entertainment keyword to bias retrieval. If the question lacks entertainment cues and does not list multiple titles, do not guess or add an entertainment disambiguator—keep the query anchored to the visible tokens only.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- When the context contains a concise bridge claim about a named entity and at least one passage provides an explicit, identity-aligned statement linking that same named entity (or a clearly indicated alias) to the asserted relation, append exactly one brief corroborating sentence that paraphrases that passage's linking fact and references the corroborating passage. Perform an explicit identity-alignment check (alias or explicit linking); do not treat mere name co-occurrence or a passage that merely mentions the name as sufficient. Do not append this corroboration if the `context` already contains the identical corroborating fact verbatim. Keep this addition short and keep the overall summary compact.
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.
- When the summaries contain both a short single-token alias and, in the later integrated summary (summary_2), a contiguous multi-token full-name literal that explicitly identifies or is explicitly linked to the same person (for example via an identity link or biographical framing in summary_2), prefer and emit that multi-token full-name literal exactly as it appears in summary_2. Do not prefer the longer form unless summary_2 provides an explicit identity link tying the alias to the full-name literal; if no such explicit link is present, follow the base identity-link rules and do not force selection of the longer form.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?
hop1_docs: Cult of Personality (song) | "Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.  It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.  "Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.  It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.  Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.  The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".  The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.  It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".  The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".  In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".  The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".  It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".  The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.  The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".

WWE: Uncaged | WWE: Uncaged is a compilation album of unreleased professional wrestling entrance theme songs which was released by WWE on December 16, 2016 on online music stores.  The album features multiple tracks that were not available to the general public before the release of the album, most notably the first version of Chris Jericho's theme song "Break the Walls Down", which was composed by Jim Johnston and performed by Adam Morenoff used for his WWE debut back in 1999.  The album also features the acoustic versions of Batista's theme song, "I Walk Alone" and Randy Orton's theme song, "Voices" which were used for their WrestleMania 30 video packages, which aired on WWE programming before WrestleMania to get fans hyped and excited for their matches at the event.

Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song) | "Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.  The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.  The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.  The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.  The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute

WWE No Mercy | No Mercy is an annual professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  The first No Mercy was held on May 16, 1999 in Manchester, England.  The event was held annually in October from 1999 to 2008, and from 2003 to 2006 it was exclusive to the SmackDown!  brand.  In 2016, the event returned as a "SmackDown" exclusive following the reinstated brand extension and aired on the WWE Network for the first time.  However in 2017, the pay-per-view will be a Raw branded event.  The song "No Mercy" by Jim Johnston was used as a regular theme song for the events in 2002, 2004, 2006, and 2007.  In the 2016 and 2017 events, the theme song was "No Mercy" by KIT.

Line in the Sand (Motörhead song) | Line in the Sand is a 2004 rock song from WWE's soundtrack album, "".  The song used to be the entrance theme of the professional wrestling alliance, Evolution.  The song is performed by Motörhead.

WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour | The WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour was a professional wrestling televised event held by World Wrestling Entertainment and which took place at the Westpac Stadium in Wellington, New Zealand on March 4, 2006.  The event was taped and later aired on WWE 24/7 in the United States.

Stadium anthem | Stadium anthems or sports anthems or arena anthems are a musical genre identifying songs that are played over the public address systems at stadiums and arenas during breaks in the action to rally the fans.  Unlike college fight songs, most stadium anthems were not written primarily for use at sports events, though compilations such as "ESPN Presents Stadium Anthems" and the ""Jock"" series occasionally feature remixed versions of these songs designed to segue together or to accentuate the rhythm or other elements of the songs.  Some football events have their own anthems, but they are not played during breaks, but they are played as entrance music.  The most notable anthems are the FIFA Anthem and the UEFA Champions League Anthem.
hop1_titles: WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour
response.summary_1: Passage [1] states "'the song is also the entrance music for ... CM Punk' and 'Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.'" Bridge entity: WrestleMania 29. Missing fact / next-hop: retrieve which New Jersey stadium hosted WrestleMania 29 (stadium name and location). Use queries like "WrestleMania 29 stadium" or "WrestleMania 29 held at which stadium in New Jersey" as evidence anchors.
hop2_docs: WrestleMania 29 | WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.  Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.

WrestleMania 31 | WrestleMania 31 was the thirty-first annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  It took place on March 29, 2015, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California.

WrestleMania IV | WrestleMania IV was the fourth annual WrestleMania sports entertainment pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).  It took place on March 27, 1988, at the Historic Atlantic City Convention Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey.  The announced attendance of WrestleMania IV was 19,199.

WrestleMania V | WrestleMania V was the fifth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) and closed-circuit TV event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).  It took place on April 2, 1989, at the Boardwalk Hall in Atlantic City, New Jersey.  The event was commentated by Gorilla Monsoon and Jesse Ventura.

WrestleMania XXVI | WrestleMania XXVI was the twenty-sixth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), which took place on March 28, 2010, at the University of Phoenix Stadium in the Phoenix suburb of Glendale, Arizona.  This was the first WrestleMania since WrestleMania XI with a non-title match as a main event, the first WrestleMania to be held in the state of Arizona and the third to be held in an open-air venue, after WrestleMania IX and WrestleMania XXIV.

WrestleMania 32 | WrestleMania 32 was the thirty-second annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view event produced by WWE.  It took place on April 3, 2016, at the AT&T Stadium in Arlington, Texas.

WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour | The WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour was a professional wrestling televised event held by World Wrestling Entertainment and which took place at the Westpac Stadium in Wellington, New Zealand on March 4, 2006.  The event was taped and later aired on WWE 24/7 in the United States.
hop2_titles: WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour
response.summary_2: WrestleMania 29 — where CM Punk used "Cult of Personality" for his entrance — was held at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey (passage [1]).
response.answer: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey
